title: 10 best herbal tinctures for oily skin

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander tinctures helps with oily skin because of its unique ability to balance the body's natural sebum production. The herb contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation and regulate oil gland activity, resulting in a decrease in excess oil production. Additionally, germander tincture's antioxidant properties help to combat environmental stressors that can exacerbate oily skin, promoting a healthy and radiant complexion. By using germander tincture topically or internally, individuals with oily skin can experience a reduction in shine and an improvement in overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Germander's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to acne and other skin issues associated with oily skin.

Antibacterial and antifungal activity: Germander's antibacterial and antifungal properties help to combat the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause acne, clogged pores, and other skin problems common in oily skin.

Astringent and styptic effects: Germander's astringent and styptic properties help to constrict blood vessels, reduce oil production, and tighten pores, which can help to regulate sebum levels and reduce the appearance of oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenes and saponins, the roots of the Germander plant help to regulate sebum production and reduce inflammation associated with oily skin.
Barks: The barks of the Germander plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating acne and reducing oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh teucrium chamaedrys leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or desiccation for 2-4 weeks.
Grind 20 grams of dried germander leaves into a fine powder and mix with 60% vodka.
Store the mixture in an amber glass jar and steep at room temperature for 14 days.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with oily skin because of its unique ability to balance the body's natural oil production. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties in sage help to reduce excessive sebum secretion, while also soothing and calming the skin. Additionally, sage's astringent properties help to tighten pores, minimizing their appearance and reducing shine. As a result, sage tinctures can effectively regulate oily skin, leaving it looking clearer, smoother, and more balanced.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Sage's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to clogged pores and acne on oily skin.
Astringent properties: Sage's astringent properties help to tighten pores and reduce sebum production, leaving the skin feeling smooth and balanced.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with acne and other skin issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, sage roots help reduce sebum production and calm inflammation in oily skin.
Leaves: Containing rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant, sage leaves are effective in reducing redness, swelling, and acne caused by oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry the salvia officinalis leaves in small batches to preserve their potency.
Grind 100 grams of dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Combine the powdered sage with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the herb is completely submerged.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 6 to 8 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another glass jar, discarding the solids and preserving the resulting tincture for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with oily skin because it has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to balance the skin's pH level, reducing excess oil production. The aloe vera gel absorbs quickly into the skin, soothing and calming clogged pores, while also regulating sebum production. Additionally, aloe tincture contains antioxidants that combat free radicals, which can cause acne and breakouts. By using herbal aloe tinctures, individuals with oily skin can experience reduced shine, smaller pores, and a more even-toned complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with oily skin, promoting a calm and balanced complexion.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents in aloe vera tincture help control the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to acne and other skin issues often linked to oily skin, preventing breakouts and infections.

Astringent and antiseptic properties: Aloe vera's astringent and antiseptic compounds help shrink pores, dry up excess oil, and kill bacteria on the skin's surface, reducing shine and improving overall skin tone.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the aloe plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce redness and irritation associated with oily skin.
Leaves: Aloe leaf gel is known for its soothing and calming properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves containing yellow sap.
Crush the harvested aloe vera gel into fine paste using mortar and pestle.
Mix the crushed aloe vera paste with 20% vodka or grain alcohol in 1:4 ratio by weight.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and store it in dark place for 14 days shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with oily skin because it has natural astringent properties that help to balance sebum production, reducing the appearance of pores and controlling excess oil. The antibacterial compounds in pot marigold also help to combat acne-causing bacteria, preventing breakouts and inflammation. Additionally, the antioxidant-rich extract soothes and calms the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin conditions, leaving the complexion looking clearer and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Pot marigold's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and eliminate bacteria that can clog pores, leading to a reduction in oil production.
Soothing and Calming Effect: The tannins present in pot marigold have a soothing and calming effect on the skin, which can help to reduce redness and irritation associated with oily skin.
Astringent Properties: Pot marigold's astringent properties help to constrict pores, reducing their size and oil output, which can lead to a more balanced and controlled skin tone.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are used to make a calming and soothing tincture that helps to reduce inflammation and redness associated with oily skin.

Seeds: The seeds contain essential fatty acids and antioxidants, making them an excellent ingredient in tinctures for oily skin, as they help to balance and regulate the skin's natural oil production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and chop them finely to release their essential oils.
Combine chopped flowers with a neutral-tasting carrier oil like sweet almond oil in a 1:3 ratio by weight.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with an airtight lid and allow it to macerate for six weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Transfer the herbal tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool, dark place for at least two weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with oily skin because of its natural astringent and antiseptic properties, which help to balance and regulate the skin's sebum production. The tannins present in dandelion roots also possess anti-inflammatory and antibacterial qualities that aid in reducing acne and redness associated with excessive oiliness. By using dandelion tinctures topically, individuals can experience a reduction in pore size, a decrease in oil production, and an overall improvement in the appearance of their skin, leaving it looking clearer and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion tincture's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to oily skin issues.

Astringent and anti-inflammatory properties: The astringent and anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion tincture can help reduce the production of sebum and calm irritated skin, making it an effective natural remedy for oily skin.
Digestive support properties: By supporting digestive health, dandelion tincture may help regulate hormones that stimulate oil production, leading to a reduction in excess oil on the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, including acne and eczema, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.

Seeds: Dandelion seeds contain a compound called sesquiterpenes, which has been shown to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried dandelion flowers and leaves at dawn or dusk to ensure maximum potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and combine it with 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, until the liquid has taken on a golden hue.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the dandelion tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for up to 2 years, keeping it out of direct sunlight and heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with oily skin because it contains rosmarinic acid, a natural antioxidant that reduces inflammation and minimizes pores. The antiseptic properties of lemon balm also help to control the production of sebum, the natural oil produced by the skin. By balancing the skin's natural pH levels, lemon balm tincture helps to regulate oil secretion, leading to a reduction in shine and a more even-toned complexion. Additionally, its antibacterial properties combat acne-causing bacteria, promoting clear and healthy-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress, leading to inflammation and oil production in the skin.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in lemon balm, such as rosmarinic acid, can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with oily skin conditions like acne and rosacea.

Astringent Properties: Lemon balm's astringent properties can help to balance the skin's natural pH levels and tighten pores, reducing oil production and leaving the skin feeling smoother and more refined.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are a popular choice for oily skin due to their ability to soothe and calm inflammation, reducing redness and irritation.
Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant contain compounds that help to regulate sebum production, making them effective in treating acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lemon balm leaves at dawn when essential oils are most concentrated for 30-40 minutes.
Chop 100g of freshly harvested lemon balm leaves into small pieces and mix with 200ml of high-proof vodka.
Transfer the chopped herb mixture to a clean glass jar and seal tightly for 4 weeks to allow steeping time.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding solids after 2 hours.
Store the resulting tincture in a cool dark place and use within 6 months, taking 20-30 drops as needed for oily skin relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping thyme tinctures helps with oily skin because of its natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help to balance the skin's pH levels and reduce excess oil production. The tincture also has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and congestion. Additionally, creeping thyme contains compounds that have astringent and toning effects, helping to tighten pores and refine the skin's texture, leaving it looking clearer and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Creeping thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol, help to combat acne-causing bacteria on the skin, reducing inflammation and preventing breakouts.
Astringent properties: Thyme's astringent properties help to tighten pores and reduce sebum production, making it an effective natural remedy for oily skin and controlling shine.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Creeping thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with acne and other skin issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of creeping thyme are used because they contain a higher concentration of volatile oils, which can help to reduce sebum production and combat acne-causing bacteria.

Buds: The buds of creeping thyme are used because they have a high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation associated with oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality fresh or dried thymus serpyllum leaves and flowers weighing approximately one ounce.
Combine the thymus serpyllum with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar covering the herb completely filling it to the brim.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks allowing the mixture to steep and extract its medicinal properties.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids after three hours.
Transfer the final tincture solution to dark glass bottles storing them in the refrigerator at 40 degrees fahrenheit for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with oily skin because of its natural ability to reduce inflammation and balance the body's hormonal levels. The herb's active compounds, such as hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to inhibit the production of sebum, leading to a decrease in oiliness. Additionally, St John's Wort has antibacterial properties that help to prevent acne-causing bacteria from thriving on the skin. As a result, using St John's Wort tinctures can lead to clearer, more balanced skin and a reduction in oily patches.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help to reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin that contribute to oily skin and acne.

Hypotensive effects: St. John's Wort tincture has been shown to have a hypotensive effect, meaning it can help to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation in the skin, which may help to alleviate oily skin symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: St. John's Wort contains antioxidants that can help to neutralize free radicals in the skin, which can damage skin cells and contribute to signs of aging, such as fine lines and wrinkles, but also oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help regulate oil production and reduce inflammation in the skin.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and hyperforin, flowers exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective against acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st. john's wort flowers and leaves in the summer months ensuring adequate quantity for drying.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or dehydrator on low heat for several hours.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces to facilitate maceration and tincture creation.
Combine 1 part chopped st. john's wort with 5 parts vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar and steep for 2 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the tincture mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding solids and transferring to dark glass bottle storing at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with oily skin because of its ability to balance and regulate sebum production. The extract from the maidenhair tree contains compounds that have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the appearance of pores and control excess oil secretion. Antioxidant properties: Maidenhair tree tincture's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and accelerate aging, helping to balance and regulate oily skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maidenhair tree roots have been traditionally used to treat acne, inflammation, and other skin issues associated with oily skin due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that help reduce sebum production and prevent clogged pores, making it effective in treating oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and wash them gently with distilled water to remove dirt.
Chop the washed ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces using scissors or a herb cutter to increase surface area.
Combine 1 part chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with 5 parts high-proof vodka in a glass jar and store in a cool dark place for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar and discard the solids after 2 hours.
Store the filtered tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with oily skin because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb, also known as Echinacea purpurea, has been traditionally used to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with acne-prone complexions. Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The tincture's antimicrobial compounds may help combat acne-causing bacteria and fungi, reducing the likelihood of clogged pores and breakouts, and promoting a healthier balance of skin flora.

Sesquiterpene content: Purple coneflower contains sesquiterpenes, which have been found to possess antiseptic and antioxidant properties that can help regulate oil production, reduce inflammation, and protect the skin from environmental stressors.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots of the purple coneflower help reduce sebum production and soothe irritated skin.
Fruits: The fruits of the plant contain anthocyanins, powerful flavonoids that possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for balancing oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea flowers in mid-summer when they are fully bloomed and dry them immediately.
Combine one part dried echinacea purpurea flowers with three parts vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 6 to 8 weeks in a cool dark place allowing the tincture to infuse slowly.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids carefully.
Store the final tincture in an amber glass bottle and label it correctly using the ingredients dosages and preparation time.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Combine the harvested herbs with 500ml of vodka in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids after 2 hours.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in a cool place for up to 24 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from the rosmarinus officinalis plant at dawn when essential oils are most concentrated for optimal potency.
Cleanly chop 200g of the harvested leaves into small pieces and add them to a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Pour 500ml of high-proof vodka over the chopped leaves in the jar, ensuring that all the plant material is completely submerged in the liquid.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion and extraction of active compounds.

Combine one part sambucus nigra flowers and leaves with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for six weeks at room temperature, shaking the jar daily to ensure proper extraction.

remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare herb and 50% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried herb and vodka, filling the jar to the top and ensuring the herb is completely covered.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks allowing for steeping and extraction.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid tincture.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with oily skin because its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties work to balance the skin's pH level, reducing excess oil production. Astringent properties: Peppermint's astringent properties help tighten pores, reducing the appearance of enlarged pores and minimizing the amount of sebum produced, leaving skin feeling smoother and more balanced.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or store-bought organic peppermint leaves in bulk quantity about 250g.
Dry the peppermint leaves using a dehydrator on low setting for 6 hours or air-dry them in a warm dark place.
Combine dried peppermint leaves with 80% ethanol and 20% water to create a 1:4 ratio by weight.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking daily, then strain through cheesecloth and discard solids.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in cool dark place for 6 months before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with oily skin because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive compound curcumin, present in turmeric, has been shown to reduce sebum production and prevent clogged pores. Additionally, it soothes and calms the skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with acne-prone skin. As a result, herbal turmeric tinctures can help regulate oil production, unclog pores, and leave the skin looking clear and balanced.

Inflammation reduction: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, can help reduce inflammation in the skin, which is often a underlying cause of oily skin and related issues like acne and redness.
Sebum regulation: The bioactive compounds in turmeric tinctures have been shown to regulate sebum production in the skin, helping to balance oil levels and reduce excess shine and greasiness.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of turmeric are rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce acne and soothe oily skin.

Barks: The inner bark of the turmeric plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it effective against acne-causing bacteria and fungi.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried turmeric root, also known as curcuma longa, and transfer it to a clean glass jar.
Combine the turmeric root with 500ml of high-proof vodka in the jar and seal it tightly.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 4 weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the tincture to mature.
After the 4-week period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Transfer the curcuma longa tincture to dark-glass dropper bottles and store them in the refrigerator for up to 2 years, ready for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail tinctures helps with oily skin because it possesses a unique blend of antioxidants, flavonoids, and silicon-rich compounds that work synergistically to balance sebum production. The tannins in horsetail help to tighten pores, reducing the appearance of oiliness, while its anti-inflammatory properties soothe acne-prone skin. Additionally, horsetail's ability to regulate hormonal imbalances can help to reduce excess oil production at the root cause, leading to a clearer and more radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Horsetail's high antioxidant content helps to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, leading to a reduction in sebum production and improvement in skin clarity.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The plant's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce redness and swelling associated with oily skin, while also soothing irritated skin and reducing the appearance of pores.

Astringent properties: Horsetail's tannins and flavonoids have astringent effects, which can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce sebum production, resulting in a more balanced skin tone and reduced oiliness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the field horsetail plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to help regulate and balance the skin's natural oil production, making it an effective ingredient in tinctures for oily skin.

Rhizomes: Field horsetail rhizomes are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh equisetum arvense plant material weighing 200-250 grams.
Dry the plant material in a warm well-ventilated area for 24 hours.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Steep the chopped plant material in 70% ethanol at a ratio of 1:5 for 14 days.
Strain and filter the tincture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for oily skin are concentrated liquid extracts made from plant-based ingredients that have been used for centuries to balance and regulate the skin's natural moisture levels. These tinctures help to reduce oil production, soothe acne-prone skin, and tighten pores, resulting in a clearer and more radiant complexion. Examples of herbal tinctures that benefit oily skin include tea tree oil, chamomile, and lavender, which can be applied topically or taken internally as a supplement. By using these tinctures, individuals with oily skin can enjoy a reduced risk of breakouts, improved confidence, and a healthier-looking glow.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily skin with herbal tinctures is to focus on ingredients that balance oil production and reduce inflammation. A blend of Burdock root, Dandelion root, and Lavender extract helps to purify the skin and regulate sebum levels. Combine equal parts of each herb in a base of witch hazel or aloe vera gel, then apply topically 2-3 times daily to achieve a healthy, balanced complexion.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily skin that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions characterized by excess oil production, clogged pores, and inflammation. These include acne, rosacea, and seborrheic dermatitis. Herbal tinctures such as tea tree oil, lavender oil, and chamomile have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to balance the skin's natural pH, reducing acne and redness while soothing irritated skin.
